page: Amazon Web Services c6g.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the c6g.2xlarge in the average tier for multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yielded a score of 12132.72, while the Geekbench multi-core score reached 5457.0. Memory read-only cached bandwidth is measured at 472.95 GB/sec, and uncached write bandwidth is 54.49 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 960.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 5241.48. Cryptographic performance is supported by hardware AES acceleration, though overall encryption benchmarks remain in the lower tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 Graviton architecture offers a resource-dense alternative to traditional x86 instances, delivering balanced multi-threaded capabilities at a lower resource overhead. The instance demonstrates solid performance in database operations, achieving 9.39 million operations per second in Redis SET tests, and static web serving throughput of 9.46 GB/sec. It is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, containerized microservices, static web serving, and distributed database nodes, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12132.72 and Redis SET throughput of 9.39 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and cryptographic operations are relatively weak. The c6g.2xlarge offers a cost-efficient, resource-dense profile for multi-threaded workloads, making it highly suitable for software compilation, static web serving, containerized microservices, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 Graviton vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, built for multi-threaded workloads and web serving.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering dedicated physical cores and balanced multi-threaded performance for containerized microservices and web serving.

16.0 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Average multi-core performance with 12132.72 stress-ng score
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and database nodes
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
